rationale: Empirically exercised the full chain at 4e8a1f45. (1) mindspec config show renders 'runner: claude-code-skills # declared, not yet enforced'; ms-panel-run's grep '^runner:' matches and its Runner-dispatch section correctly branches workflow-path vs skills-path. (2) Built the branch binary; in a /tmp scratch repo ran the exact commands buildCommand assembles: 'mindspec panel create <slug> --spec <id> --target <ref> --round 1' stamped panel.json correctly (round, reviewed_head_sha, expected_reviewers=6, approve_threshold=n-1); 'panel verify'/'panel tally' returned coherent stdout. (3) Install scope: 'mindspec setup claude' installs .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js; 'mindspec setup copilot' installs nothing under .agents/ or .github/ — confirmed empirically and TestClaudeSetup_InstallsWorkflowClaudeTargetOnly passes; .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js is byte-identical to plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js (TestWorkflowFiles_EmbedsMsPanel passes). (4) Re-ran the RCE attack: extracted the actual pre-agent() validateArgs/buildCommand source verbatim into a /tmp node harness and drove it with the BRIEF's payloads plus extras (path traversal, control bytes, git-ref-disallowed constructs) across slug/spec/bead_id/target/round/mix — all throw; 'mindspec complete' appears 0 times in ms-panel.js; TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet passes. (5) The plan's Manual e2e shim scenarios are explicitly documented as requiring the live Claude Code agent()/pipeline() runtime and are 'NOT automatable in CI' — genuinely infeasible for me to drive as a subagent without that runtime; I substituted the closest empirical equivalent (manually running the exact CLI commands each agent step issues), which held together end to end.
findings: Empirical confirmation: 'mindspec config show | grep ^runner:' matches the config's rendered line exactly as ms-panel-run's Runner-dispatch step requires; default remains claude-code-skills.

Empirical confirmation: the full mindspec panel create -> verify -> tally round trip (the exact commands ms-panel.js's buildCommand constructs for Steps 2 and 5) works in a scratch repo and produces a correctly-stamped panel.json and coherent verify/tally output.

Empirical confirmation: mindspec setup installs .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js only for the Claude target; mindspec setup copilot installs no equivalent workflow file. TestClaudeSetup_InstallsWorkflowClaudeTargetOnly and TestWorkflowFiles_EmbedsMsPanel both pass, and a direct diff shows the .claude/ and plugins/mindspec/ copies of ms-panel.js are byte-identical.

Empirical RCE re-attack (extracted validateArgs/buildCommand verbatim, ran in a /tmp node vm sandbox): x$(id), x;id, x|sh, x`whoami`, foo bar, path traversal, control bytes, and git-ref-disallowed target constructs all throw before any command string is built; 'mindspec complete' occurs 0 times in the file; TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet passes.

Minor non-blocking observation: validateArgs's own field-level checks do not reject a leading-dash value for slug/spec/bead_id (only validateTarget has an explicit leading-dash check at that layer) — but buildCommand's separate, generic leading-dash guard runs over every value on every call and always executes downstream of validateArgs in the workflow body (line 452 then 462), so a leading-dash slug/spec/bead_id is still rejected before any command is assembled. Confirmed empirically: buildCommand(CMD_PANEL_CREATE, '-flag', ...) throws 'rejected a leading-dash value'. No exploitable gap in the actual pipeline; the guard just lives in a different function than the other value checks, worth a comment note for a future reader but not a fix.

The plan's 'Manual e2e' shim scenarios require the live Claude Code agent()/pipeline() dynamic-workflow runtime and are explicitly documented as not automatable in CI; not executable from this review subagent's tool surface. Substituted equivalent: manually drove the underlying CLI chain (panel create/verify/tally) the workflow's agent steps issue, which behaved correctly.
